content:
@Gary Guo suggested that we could track the fields that are used in the init! macro and not make their creation unconditional to better support packed structs: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/issues/98

We got an implementation PR last month: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/pull/106 that does a very basic syntactic check. But as I noted on the PR, we have both false positives and false negatives with that approach. So I'm not too sure if we really want to proceed that way. Any particular opinions on this matter?
Actually, thinking about this again, do we really want to support unaligned fields? Is our implementation even sound with respect to them?
We use ptr.write() to initialize fields, and this is not okay for unaligned fields. I think we should just outright ban this usage (and declare that disable_initialized_field_access is unsound)?
indeed. another fix would be that we just use write_unaligned instead when the attribute is enabled
and then we can rename it to #[packed] or something like it
I'd imagine in most cases packed structs would usually small enough that in-place-init wouldn't be necessary
I had someone request this feature IIRC
https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/blob/asahi-6.17.9-1/drivers/gpu/drm/asahi/initdata.rs#L722C43-L729 looks like this struct can just be initialized by move
oh and with better smart pointer support, we could even support creations of UnalignedRefMut<'a, T> instead of &'a mut T
For most packed use cases you can simply replace non-naturally-aligned fields with Unalign<T>

Benno Lossin said:

indeed. another fix would be that we just use write_unaligned instead when the attribute is enabled

This requires a new trait hierarchy with UnalignedInit, UnalignedPinInit, though, which I think is to much for a niche scenario.

Benno Lossin said:

I had someone request this feature IIRC

https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251206170214.GE1097212@robin.jannau.net/

Gary Guo said:

Benno Lossin said:

indeed. another fix would be that we just use write_unaligned instead when the attribute is enabled

This requires a new trait hierarchy with UnalignedInit, UnalignedPinInit, though, which I think is to much for a niche scenario.

oh yeah you're right...

Benno Lossin said:

Benno Lossin said:

I had someone request this feature IIRC

https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251206170214.GE1097212@robin.jannau.net/

Yeah, I know, which is why I am inspecting Asahi's packed structs
oh I see, didn't draw the connection
okay so we'll have to forbid packed structs
I will prepare a patch & PR
so before pin-init commit db96c51 (and linux commit 42415d1) packed structs worked in pin-init, so we'll have to submit a fix for that as well
any ideas aside from backporting the guard accessor commit?
to me that seems the most plausible
Backporting that sounds reasonable to me. Perhaps also include a patch that adds a comment saying that this is needed for soundness and backport that too.
I'd just write a new commit description at the top & otherwise keep it the same
now just to figure out which stable trees need it :)
I do think we need to document in the code that the reference creation is now needed for soundness, not just a helpful feature
oops I skipped right over that, yeah agreed
I also just confirmed that packed structs worked in all prior versions of pin-init prior to that commit
